text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-on, mega-cap tech led, metals puked, breadth narrow. Indices pushed to fresh highs with SPY +0.79% and QQQ +0.71% despite RSP lagging at +0.36%, underscoring continued mega-cap dominance. Options data with SPY PCR_vol 2.04 and QQQ PCR_vol 1.60 shows heavy protection demand rather than outright complacency, keeping chase-y but cautious flows into AI and chip leadership. Cross-asset weakness in SLV -4.84% and GLD -0.76% alongside UUP +0.40% and modest IEF -0.06% losses framed the move as an equity-specific risk-on driven by tech and freight rather than broad reflation.

INDICES — VIX closed 18.0 vs 20dMA 18.1 and was effectively flat. Daily performance ranked SPY +0.79%, DIA +0.74%, QQQ +0.71%, IWM +0.63% from best to worst. STYLE showed LARGE > SMALL by 0.15pp with large +0.79% and small +0.64%, signaling modest large-cap leadership. Most notable cross-asset move was SLV -4.84% versus GLD -0.76% and UUP +0.40%, highlighting precious-metals stress against a firmer dollar.

VOLUME — SPY volume ran roughly inline at ~1.0x while QQQ volume also printed ~1.0x, confirming steady but not climactic participation in the tech-led grind. Volume breadth showed DIA 1.1x and RSP 1.1x gaining attention while IWM 0.6x and IJR 0.6x were ignored, indicating low-conviction small-cap buying. Sector flows had XLF 1.1x, XLB 1.1x, and XLK 1.0x elevated with prices up, signaling accumulation in financials, materials, and tech while XLE 0.6x and XLP 0.6x with price up flagged lower-conviction bids there.

SECTORS — XLK Technology +1.50%, XLE Energy +0.76%, XLF Financials +0.59%, XLI Industrials +0.51%, XLU Utilities +0.51%, XLP Staples +0.31%, XLC Comms +0.30%, XLY Discretionary -0.04%, XLV Healthcare -0.05%, XLRE Real Estate -0.68%, XLB Materials -0.75%. Top industries were WGMI +4.53% and CIBR +3.31%, while bottom industries were SLV -4.84% and REMX -6.03%.

THE 7s — Top 5 baskets were Freight +3.20%, Software +2.36%, Buildout +2.13%, Cloud +1.20%, Insurance +0.89%. Bottom 5 baskets were Health -0.25%, Defense -0.73%, Old Metals -1.60%, New Metals -3.62%, Toll Road +0.01%. Strongest names sat in Freight with JBHT strength implied by Freight +3.20% and in Software with AI-linked leaders, while weakest contributors were metals names inside New Metals and Old Metals, showing clear divergence between AI and old-economy materials.

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC is June 17 with CME probabilities at hold 100%, cut 0%, hike 0% and the stance tally Hawk 6, Neutral 5, Dove 4, keeping policy effectively on a hawkish hold. USD liquidity stress printed 35/100 labeled Normal, with Reserve Scarcity at the 79th percentile as the most-stressed component. TED spread at 0.09% and ON-RRP at $2.0B signaled benign front-end funding conditions.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR_vol 2.04 and QQQ PCR_vol 1.60 with positive skew (SPY +8.1%, QQQ +10.4%) show elevated downside hedging despite record equity levels. SPY spot near 7,444 versus GEX_flip 746.0 (adjusted scale) and QQQ spot near 645 versus GEX_flip 645.0 put QQQ essentially on its gamma flip, meaning dealer hedging can amplify any intraday reversal. VIX at 18.0 vs its 20dMA 18.1 and MOVE at 71.7 vs 20dMA 70.2 indicate moderate but contained vol across equities and Treasuries.

CROSS-ASSET — Dollar strength with UUP +0.40% alongside SLV -4.84% and GLD -0.76% marked a sharp divergence between risk-on equities and pressured precious metals. Oil stability with USO +0.68% and OVX 75.7 plus HY softness with HYG -0.08% showed energy and credit not fully validating the growth optimism priced into XLK +1.50% and the Semi 7 +0.61%.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed at 52.6/100 sat squarely Neutral. Top individual movers included Cisco +13.38% (Technology), Nvidia +4.40% (Semi 7), and JBHT implied strong within Freight +3.20%. On 20d momentum, best sector was XLK +18.08% and worst was XLU -3.13%, while best basket was Semi +40.66% and worst was Medtech -11.61%. Vol spikes in BA 2.6x with price -4.73% within Defense -0.73% signaled distribution on stock-specific risk, while IJT 3.0x with price +0.60% suggested accumulation in small-cap growth.

WATCH — BA -4.73% deserves attention as a 2.6x vol spike within a weak Defense -0.73% basket could trigger further de-risking or event headlines. SLV -4.84% with ATM_IV 64.4% and skew -4.0% plus 5.9x volume in SLX -0.41% argues for monitoring metals for potential capitulation or short-term snapback. QQQ +0.71% sitting on its GEX_flip 645.0 with VXN 24.1 and strong AI flows via Semi +0.61% makes Nasdaq fragility to any macro or Fed headline worth close tracking tomorrow.
